sections.paragraph: πλάνη, ης, ἡ [IE] fem. of ὁ πλάνος, ‘a wandering/roaming from the standard route’, in imagery of veering away from what is true or right deviation, error Ro 1:27; 2 Th 2:11; 2 Pt 2:18; 3:17; 1 J 4:6; Jd 11; ἐκ πλάνης through error 1 Th 2:3; difft. ἐκ πλάνης ὁδοῦ αὐτοῦ from the error of his way = from wandering off his (proper) path Js 5:20; w. focus on intent to divert another from truth deceit Mt 27:64; Eph 4:14.
